front: Short Story: the tour guide Mr. Kapasi saves Bobby from monkeys; his address on a slip of paper blows away in the wind
Short Story: Mr. Kapasi interprets Gujarati in a doctor's office; asks Mrs. Das if she feels pain or guilt after her confession about Bobby
Short Story: an estranged couple share secrets during power outages; she reveals she signed a new lease; he reveals their stillborn child was a boy
Short Story: a married Indian man whispers the title word at the Mapparium; a boy defines it as 'loving someone you don't know'
Novel: the protagonist is named after a Russian writer because his father survived a train crash clutching a page from 'The Overcoat'
Novel: the protagonist changes his name to Nikhil at Yale and later divorces Moushumi
Novel: one brother joins the Naxalite movement and is shot dead by police; the other moves to Rhode Island and raises the dead brother's daughter
Short Story: a woman crashes into a telephone pole while trying to buy fish and stops taking care of the boy Eliot

front: Short Story: a 103-year-old woman exclaims 'She's a perfect lady!' and makes her tenant praise the moon landing as 'Splendid'
back: The Third and Final Continent (Jhumpa Lahiri)
work: Other Stories from Interpreter of Maladies

front: Author: moved to Rome and began writing exclusively in Italian; compared Italian to 'cradling a newborn' and English to a 'hairy, smelly teenager'
Short Story: a widower visits his daughter in Seattle; she discovers his relationship with Mrs. Bagchi when a postcard falls out; titled from Hawthorne's 'The Custom-House'
